question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: parse(T streamFile)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted stream file with a null getPreviousHash() value into the parse pipeline, exploiting the fact that shouldParse() calls expectedHash.contentEquals(actualHash) without null-guarding expectedHash, causing a NullPointerException that halts the parser and permanently stalls Hashgraph history ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/StreamFileParser.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getLast()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a condition where getLast() returns null (e.g., by ensuring streamFileRepository.findLatest() returns empty and the in-memory last AtomicReference is null), causing shouldParse() to skip all hash chain verification and accept any tampered stream file as the genesis file, permanently corrupting the Hashgraph history anchor?
